reference: What brings you in today? Yeah, I brought my son here. Um uh yeah, I'm I'm currently looking at him and he doesn't uh look great. While the uh while the doctor's um taking his vitals and examining the baby, I'll just ask you a couple of questions. Sure So you said that all of this started about a day ago. How was he doing before that? He was fine before that, he had like a an infection, like some sort of viral infection recently, like about a week back, he had like a runny nose and a cough, but that went away. Uh he has been very irritable and fussy. Has he been sleeping OK? I um no you know what last night he didn't sleep well at all. He was crying the whole night. Did did you have GBS or gestational diabetes? No. No, OK. Did you have any viral infections during that time, during pregnancy? No, no. Was he born via Caesarean section or uh C-section or a vaginal birth? Uh vaginal. OK. Any complications during birth? Um he's been growing and gaining weight OK. Any um developmental issues? No. OK, alright, and any medical conditions that run in the family? Uh no, just like some heart issues. Um OK, and uh does he, I forgot to ask, has he had any hospitalizations in the past? No. Any surgeries? No surgeries. Uh has he been on any medications? Any antibiotics recently? No. OK. Has um has he been vaccinated? Uh has his vaccinations, immunizations up to date? Yeah, they're all up to date so far. OK, alright, that's that's good. Um OK, and does he have any allergies that you're aware of? Pardon me? Any allergies? Oh, uh no allergies. OK alright um well it looks like um I, it's great that you brought him into the emergency room. Um if he hasn't been eating, drinking well, hasn't slept well, has been fussy, and and has been lethargic and tired, uh and is having difficulty swallowing food, um that's definitely very um serious. Uh we'll have a look inside his mouth and see what's going on. It could be that he has an infection or an inflammation of some sort. Um given his fever, he probably has an infection, um and uh we'll likely start him on antibiotics and put in an IV drip just to get in get some fluids inside him, just to get him hydrated, uh and monitor him very closely. We'll also check his vitals and um we'll do uh, yeah, we'll go from there. Do you have any questions? Uh no, thank you. OK.
